figures.caption: Fig. 6: Qualitative Comparison. Ground-truth trajectories are shown in green and model predictions in blue.
Fig. 5: Scenes selected for the main quantitative results on HQ-EVFI. Scenes with highly dynamic motion are chosen to evaluate models under complex real-world motion.
Fig. 9: Qualitative comparison of video frame interpolation.
Fig. 1: Training data scale of eventbased trackers.
Fig. 4: Overall architecture. The concentration network aggregates multi-scale events into a 3-channel representation. Pseudo-labels for tracking and optical flow are provided by the teacher tracker.
Fig. 7: Qualitative comparison under dynamic motion.

Fig. 10: Qualitative comparison of video frame interpolation ablations. (A) is the baseline model without any conditional signal, (B) adds Fwarp, and (C) adds Lattn and adds Mevent for final TETO-VFI. Each result corresponds to the respective entry in the ablation table.
